zh.Essex, Essex, Massachusetts Bay, British: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可艾可

nameMeanings.name: Edwards
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Edward , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: One of the earliest American bearers of this very common English surname was William Edwards, the son of Rev. Richard Edwards, a London clergyman in the age of Elizabeth I, who came to New England c. 1640. His descendant Jonathan (1703–58), of East Windsor, CT, was a prominent Congregational clergyman whose New England theology led to the first Great Awakening, a great religious revival.

variants.name: Edward
meanings: English and Welsh: from the Middle English personal name Edward (Old English Ēadweard, from ēad ‘prosperity, fortune’ + w(e)ard ‘guard, ward’). The popularity of the personal name was largely due to its being the name of the sainted Anglo-Saxon king Edward the Confessor and of three kings in the 13th–14th centuries.

nameMeanings.name: Frances
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Francis .  In the 16th century the two spellings were used indiscriminately for both sexes, the distinction in spelling not being established until the 17th century.

en.lifeSummary: Frances Edwards was born about 1560. She had at least 1 daughter with Robert Hawes. She died before 10 September 1645, in Essex, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.Essex, Essex, Massachusetts Bay, British: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 에세스
ko.lifeSummary: Frances Edwards(은)는 대략 1560년 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert Hawes(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1645년 9월 10일 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 에세스에서 사망했다.
